title: Make it easier to override git variables
body:
Use $VERSION instead of the "$CIRRUS_TAG".
(the other variable to set is $GIT_COMMIT)

Avoid doing an unnecessary `git fetch --tags`.
Move the NUMBER sed hacks to the shell script.

Allows building from e.g. tarball, without git.

Also allows building within a _different_ git.

Example use:

```shell
make VERSION=v1.7.0 GIT_COMMIT=b7ce1157b00af09f4a09e39b377aa3abff46ee05
```

Closes #4787

Hi @afbjorklund. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

It would still be easier if there was a VERSION file in the repository, updated by the release script ?

This is used in other projects, such as conmon: `VERSION := $(shell cat VERSION)`

Then there is no risk of the user giving wrong information, and also the README can be updated:

`* [Latest Version: 1.6.3](https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/latest)`

----

Too many manual steps now, and easy to miss one:

```go
// Version is the version of the build.
// NOTE: remember to bump the version at the top
// of the top-level README.md file when this is
// bumped.
const Version = "1.7.0"
```

Although this was mostly about the git clone requirement.


/assign @vrothberg

> Cirrus isn't yet happy. I assume there are some leftover references in `contrib/cirrus/lib.sh`.

Pretty sure that I did not touch $RELEASE_ARCH at all.

```
[+0000s] Parsing actual_release.txt contents: X-RELEASE-INFO: podman  fedora 30 amd64
[+0000s] ************************************************
[+0000s] >>>>> FATAL: main() requires $RELEASE_ARCH to be non-empty
[+0000s] ************************************************
```

But it seems that it is indeed missing the $RELEASE_VERSION

Probably missing an `export` ? EDIT: right, "export" is just for make
Definitely made it _worse_ with this change, so closing for now.

FWIW: There are a bunch of values that come from `release.txt` which is produced by the Makefile.  I had a lot of trouble getting this stuff working across both Fedora and Ubuntu, so it's easily possible there is an excessive amount of duct-tape :confused: 

I do like your motivations in this PR though, maybe try doing them one at a time instead of all at once.  IIRC, cirrus doesn't fetch tag info at all when cloning repos.  But that info. isn't needed until the build/upload release process happens.  Both of those have their own (obviously) named scripts under `contrib/cirrus/` if you want to hard-code a `git fetch` or two into them instead of the Makefile, that's fine by me.

HTH and thanks for making this effort.

I noticed in the issue that the Fedora and Ubuntu packages are still missing build information, like git commit and build time. So it is not _easy_ to get this right. Haven't checked the [opensuse](https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable) packages yet...

podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64.rpm
```json
{
    "RemoteAPIVersion": 1,
    "Version": "1.6.2",
    "GoVersion": "go1.13.1",
    "GitCommit": "",
    "Built": 0,
    "OsArch": "linux/amd64"
}
```

podman_1.6.2-1\~ubuntu16.04\~ppa1_amd64.deb
```json
{
    "RemoteAPIVersion": 1,
    "Version": "1.6.2",
    "GoVersion": "go1.10.4",
    "GitCommit": "",
    "Built": 0,
    "OsArch": "linux/amd64"
}
```
I agree it's very much not easy, I spent a very long time tinkering with this kind of thing.

Actually, it _used_ to be easy. You would do your `./configure` and then your `make dist`
(and the version information would be saved from the version control system into the tarball)

ya, and then some dumb humans went in and added some "Features" :grinning: 

I'm happy to help advise from the automation/cirrus/scripts perspective, just mention me or ping me on irc.  If it would help, I'm open to extending the fields in `release.txt` if that might make things easier.  AFAIK, that file is only consumed by one or two scripts that should be easy to update.
